title: Runtime integration compatibility matrix and test-harness specification
preview:
Summary
-------
This document defines a standardized compatibility matrix for runtime components (kernels, sharders, kv-cache, dispatcher, and adapter plugins) and the accompanying test-harness specification used by the eng-serving-runtime team for validation before canary and GA rollouts. The goal is to reduce unde...
text:
Summary
-------
This document defines a standardized compatibility matrix for runtime components (kernels, sharders, kv-cache, dispatcher, and adapter plugins) and the accompanying test-harness specification used by the eng-serving-runtime team for validation before canary and GA rollouts. The goal is to reduce undetected integration regressions by codifying deterministic tests, telemetry contracts, and release gating criteria.

Why this exists
----------------
- Multiple runtime subsystems evolve independently (kernel optimizations, scheduler policy changes, third-party accelerator adapters). We need a repeatable, automated way to validate interop.
- Current ad-hoc checks miss cross-layer regressions that only surface under mixed workloads or long-lived sessions.
- This spec provides a compatibility matrix format, a test-harness architecture, canonical test suites, and a release gate...

title: Slot-abort Contract and Graceful Recovery Protocol
preview:
SUMMARY

This document specifies the Slot-abort Contract and Graceful Recovery Protocol used by the serving runtime scheduler and kernel plugins. It formalizes the signals, invariants, telemetry, operator runbook, and verification tests required for safe slot abortion, state preservation, and deterministic recovery ...
text:
SUMMARY

This document specifies the Slot-abort Contract and Graceful Recovery Protocol used by the serving runtime scheduler and kernel plugins. It formalizes the signals, invariants, telemetry, operator runbook, and verification tests required for safe slot abortion, state preservation, and deterministic recovery paths. The goal is to minimize user-visible SLO impact during kernel-level preemption, eviction, or forced termination while keeping the runtime auditable and testable.

INTENDED AUDIENCE
- Runtime kernel and scheduler engineers
- Oncall and SRE teams
- Release engineers validating scheduler behavior
- Performance and profiling teams

GOALS AND NON-GOALS
Goals:
- Define a single contract for how a running request slot may be aborted or suspended.
- Ensure deterministic state handoff for recoverable requests (KV cache, partial logits, decoding state).
- Provide telemetry and...

title: SLA-aware Admission Controller and Backpressure Playbook
preview:
Summary:

This playbook defines the SLA-aware Admission Controller and Backpressure strategy for Redwood's serving runtime. It describes the design, configuration, operational runbook, and recommended testing/benchmarking steps to ensure tenant-level SLOs are respected under mixed workloads and overload scenarios. T...
text:
Summary:

This playbook defines the SLA-aware Admission Controller and Backpressure strategy for Redwood's serving runtime. It describes the design, configuration, operational runbook, and recommended testing/benchmarking steps to ensure tenant-level SLOs are respected under mixed workloads and overload scenarios. The playbook targets runtime-admission (service), kernel-scheduler (service), and dispatcher components and is intended for on-call, runtime engineers, and SREs.

Goals and scope:

- Ensure per-tenant and per-route latency SLOs are prioritized when system capacity is constrained.
- Provide deterministic admission behavior that is auditable and testable.
- Define backpressure signals exposed to clients and internal services.
- Offer a practical operational runbook for detecting, mitigating, and rolling back admission policy changes.

Out of scope:

- Model-level quality regre...

title: Causal Latency Attribution and Signal Enrichment Playbook
preview:
Summary and purpose

This playbook defines a repeatable approach for attributing causal sources of request latency in the serving runtime and enriching traces and metrics with synthesized signals that improve root-cause diagnosis. The intended audience is runtime engineers, SREs, and profiling/product teams who inve...
text:
Summary and purpose

This playbook defines a repeatable approach for attributing causal sources of request latency in the serving runtime and enriching traces and metrics with synthesized signals that improve root-cause diagnosis. The intended audience is runtime engineers, SREs, and profiling/product teams who investigate tail-latency incidents or validate scheduler/kernel changes against real traffic.

Goals
- Produce an actionable causal hypothesis for >80% of investigated tail-latency incidents within 48 hours.
- Ensure enriched telemetry is available for post-incident analysis and automated detection rules within 6 hours of rollout.
- Keep incremental instrumentation cost increase under 12% of telemetry budget for a standard region.

Scope and non-goals
- Scope: production serving runtime (request surface -> kernel dispatch -> GPU execution -> response), associated request routin...

title: Observability → Cost Signal Mapping and Ownership Roster
preview:
Overview:
This document defines a pragmatic mapping between runtime observability signals and the cost / SLO consequences they imply. It consolidates ownership for each signal, provides concrete alert thresholds, investigative playbooks, and sample queries to accelerate incident triage and cost attribution. The goal...
text:
Overview:
This document defines a pragmatic mapping between runtime observability signals and the cost / SLO consequences they imply. It consolidates ownership for each signal, provides concrete alert thresholds, investigative playbooks, and sample queries to accelerate incident triage and cost attribution. The goal is to make cost-impacting signals actionable for oncall, SREs, runtime engineers, and product stakeholders without repetitive context-switching.

Why this exists:
- We observe recurring incidents where telemetry is present but responsibility and cost mapping are unclear.
- Kernel-level events (slow kernels, preemption, fragmentation) generate telemetry that must be translated into cost signals (excess GPU-hours, increased tail-latency retries, failed inference units).
- This doc standardizes naming, ownership, and initial remediation steps so pager fatigue and finger-point...

title: Quantization-Induced Timing Investigation Handbook
preview:
Summary

This handbook codifies a standard investigation and mitigation workflow for latency regressions and high-variance tail behavior that correlate with quantized model kernels or mixed-precision execution paths. It is targeted at SREs, runtime engineers, and applied ML engineers responsible for rollout safeties...
text:
Summary

This handbook codifies a standard investigation and mitigation workflow for latency regressions and high-variance tail behavior that correlate with quantized model kernels or mixed-precision execution paths. It is targeted at SREs, runtime engineers, and applied ML engineers responsible for rollout safeties and kernel scheduling.

Scope

- Applies to inference serving instances using int8/4/2 quantization paths, mixed FP16/FP32 kernels, and runtime kernel switching (auto, fallback, or staged rollout).
- Covers production incidents and reproducible lab investigations.
- Excludes model-quality hallucination investigation (see quality-guardrails space).

Symptoms that should trigger this playbook

1) Sudden increase in p99/p999 latency after a quantization rollout or kernel update.
2) Increased variance in kernel execution durations in kernel-duration histograms.
3) Elevated ker...

title: Adaptive Telemetry Bandwidth Management and Trace Priority Playbook
preview:
Summary:
This playbook defines the Adaptive Telemetry Bandwidth Management (ATBM) policy and Trace Priority model used by the Serving Runtime to keep observability useful under constrained telemetry budgets. It covers objectives, priority classes, runtime admission & backpressure behavior, operational controls, inci...
text:
Summary:
This playbook defines the Adaptive Telemetry Bandwidth Management (ATBM) policy and Trace Priority model used by the Serving Runtime to keep observability useful under constrained telemetry budgets. It covers objectives, priority classes, runtime admission & backpressure behavior, operational controls, incident runbook, benchmark validation steps, rollout checklist, and known caveats.

Goals:
- Ensure high-value traces and metrics remain available when telemetry bandwidth is constrained.
- Keep telemetry costs bounded while preserving the signal required for production debugging and SLA forensics.
- Provide deterministic runtime behavior and simple operational levers for on-call and SRE teams.

Background:
Serving runtime produces three telemetry surfaces: kernel traces (per-kernel microtraces), request-execution traces (end-to-end per-request spans), and aggregated metrics/d...

title: async-kernel-deadlock-patterns-and-mitigation-2026
preview:
Summary:

This document catalogs recurring async-kernel deadlock patterns observed in the runtime between 2024–2026, diagnostic signatures, short-term mitigations, and durable remediation paths. It is intended for oncall engineers, runtime kernel authors, scheduler owners, and SREs.

Scope and impact:
- Affects the ...
text:
Summary:

This document catalogs recurring async-kernel deadlock patterns observed in the runtime between 2024–2026, diagnostic signatures, short-term mitigations, and durable remediation paths. It is intended for oncall engineers, runtime kernel authors, scheduler owners, and SREs.

Scope and impact:
- Affects the serving runtime's asynchronous kernel handoff and cooperative preemption paths.
- Symptoms range from elevated tail latency (p99.99 spikes) to full request stalls on specific model variants, sometimes causing partial loss of capacity on a GPU node for minutes.
- Customers: observed primarily under mixed workloads (long-context chat + short embeddings) and during kernel rollouts or topology changes.
- Business impact: can exceed alerting SLOs (p95 latency > 2x target) and trigger failover cascades in the orchestrator.

High-level patterns (observed signatures):
1) Handoff-st...

title: Degradation Probing and Load‑Shed Orchestration Manual
preview:
Overview
========
This document describes the design, operational contracts, and runbook for Redwood's runtime-level degradation probing and load‑shed orchestration subsystem (the Shedder + Probe Controller). It explains how the system detects overload or fidelity regressions, safely degrades throughput/quality, pro...
text:
Overview
========
This document describes the design, operational contracts, and runbook for Redwood's runtime-level degradation probing and load‑shed orchestration subsystem (the Shedder + Probe Controller). It explains how the system detects overload or fidelity regressions, safely degrades throughput/quality, probes for capacity recovery, and executes fallbacks to preserve customer‑facing SLOs. Intended audience: SREs, runtime engineers, oncall responders, and anyone running canary/rollout experiments against the serving runtime.

Scope and goals
----------------
- Scope: host runtime components that make admission and fidelity decisions (shedder, probe controller, router policies, kernel scheduler hooks). Includes metrics, alerting, config knobs, and incident procedures. Does NOT cover dedicated customer‑side capacity contracts or API client behavior.
- Goals:
 - Keep p99 tail lat...

title: Kernel ABI & Quantization Kernel Compatibility Guidelines
preview:
Summary

This document describes the compatibility matrix and operational guidelines for kernel ABIs and quantization-optimized kernels used by the Redwood serving runtime. It covers version mapping, supported GPU microarchitectures, recommended fallback strategies, preflight tests, safe rollout and rollback procedu...
text:
Summary

This document describes the compatibility matrix and operational guidelines for kernel ABIs and quantization-optimized kernels used by the Redwood serving runtime. It covers version mapping, supported GPU microarchitectures, recommended fallback strategies, preflight tests, safe rollout and rollback procedures, and required telemetry for forensic analysis. The goal is to make kernel selection predictable across hosted, dedicated, and private deployments and to reduce upgrade risk for private (VPC / on-prem) customers.

Scope

- Kernel ABIs exposed by the runtime (cuda/cudnn/cuBLAS, ROCm, oneAPI-compatible kernels)
- Quantization-aware kernels (int8, int4, dynamic-float16 paths) and heuristics that select them
- Compatibility across accelerator generations (NVIDIA Ampere, Hopper, Ada; AMD CDNA2/CDNA3; Intel Gaudi/ARC where applicable)
- Runtime-level rollback and canary test p...

title: Eviction Rewind and State Reconstruction Playbook
preview:
Overview
========
This playbook documents the procedure to recover from kvcache eviction events that cause request-level state divergence or incomplete KV reconstruction during model swaps, preemption, or kernel handoffs. It covers triage, automated rewind and state reconstruction action sequences, verification chec...
text:
Overview
========
This playbook documents the procedure to recover from kvcache eviction events that cause request-level state divergence or incomplete KV reconstruction during model swaps, preemption, or kernel handoffs. It covers triage, automated rewind and state reconstruction action sequences, verification checks, rollback criteria, and safety tests. The intended audience is oncall SREs, serving-runtime engineers, and incident responders.

Why this matters
-----------------
- Eviction-induced state gaps can lead to incorrect completions, user-facing hallucinations, or repeated model fallbacks.
- Fast, deterministic reconstruction preserves SLOs and avoids large-scale reroutes to lower-fidelity fallback models.
- This playbook provides a safe, repeatable sequence to reconstruct per-session KV state and safely resume traffic.

When to run this playbook
-------------------------
Run...

title: Fallback validation and chaos test plan for graceful runtime fallbacks
preview:
Purpose
-------
This document defines a repeatable validation and chaos-testing plan for the runtime's graceful fallback paths. It is intended to: verify correctness and SLA-preservation when primary kernels, model variants, or regions become unavailable; exercise fallthrough policies and automated rollback; and val...
text:
Purpose
-------
This document defines a repeatable validation and chaos-testing plan for the runtime's graceful fallback paths. It is intended to: verify correctness and SLA-preservation when primary kernels, model variants, or regions become unavailable; exercise fallthrough policies and automated rollback; and validate telemetry/alert fidelity used for automated mitigation. The goal is to catch correctness, performance regressions, and observability gaps before customer impact.

Scope
-----
- Components: kernel selector, request router, model-variant manager, KV cache coordination, shard handoff logic, admission controller, and routing fallbacks (region/model/tier).
- Test goals: functional correctness (response validity), latency SLO preservation (p50/p95/p99 targets), cost/capacity boundary behavior, and signal hygiene (trace/span/metric availability).
- Exclusions: hardware-level...

title: Runtime 1.22 Release Notes: Workload-aware batching defaults (Fast / Standard / Cost-efficient tiers)
preview:
## Overview
Runtime **v1.22** introduces **workload-aware batching defaults**: three customer-facing batching profiles aligned to latency SLO tiers (**fast**, **standard**, **cost-efficient**). The runtime now selects and enforces tier-appropriate scheduler knobs using workload signals (prompt-length buckets, stream...
text:
## Overview
Runtime **v1.22** introduces **workload-aware batching defaults**: three customer-facing batching profiles aligned to latency SLO tiers (**fast**, **standard**, **cost-efficient**). The runtime now selects and enforces tier-appropriate scheduler knobs using workload signals (prompt-length buckets, streaming vs non-streaming, and concurrency) to improve throughput and $/token while preserving predictable tail latency.

This release includes:
- New **tiered default profiles** and a **selection/override hierarchy**
- **Tail-latency protections** for fast tier and guardrails for cost-efficient tier
- **Telemetry + reason codes** to explain “why this profile”
- **Tier-labeled metrics** with **cardinality controls**
- Rollout controls: **feature flag**, staged enablement, and kill switch

## What’s new
### 1) Three batching tiers (fast / standard / cost-efficient)
The runtime no...

Summary:
This document is a consolidated experiment matrix and validation checklist for cross-layer runtime experiments that touch kernel scheduling, KV-cache behavior, profiling instrumentation, and benchmark acceptance. Use this as the canonical checklist and runbook for planning, executing, and validating experiments that modify scheduler policies, kernel variants, or observation sampling rates.

Scope and audience:
- Scope: experiments affecting serving runtime execution path, kernel selection, batching, KV cache prefetch, and telemetry sampling.
- Audience: runtime engineers, SREs, performance researchers, release engineers.

High-level experiment categories (matrix):
1) Scheduling policy changes
 - Example: priority-driven crediting -> opportunistic slot coalescing

title: Batching SLO Tier Benchmark Methodology
preview:
## Overview
This document defines the benchmark methodology used to validate the three customer-facing batching profiles (Fast / Standard / Cost-efficient) prior to rollout and on an ongoing basis via perf canary gates.

The methodology is designed to:
- Prevent “benchmark theater” by using representative mixes (pro...
text:
## Overview
This document defines the benchmark methodology used to validate the three customer-facing batching profiles (Fast / Standard / Cost-efficient) prior to rollout and on an ongoing basis via perf canary gates.

The methodology is designed to:
- Prevent “benchmark theater” by using representative mixes (prompt length, streaming ratio, and concurrency) that approximate real production traffic.
- Make regressions actionable by standardizing metrics, run configurations, and pass/fail gates.
- Provide apples-to-apples comparisons between tier candidates and existing defaults, while controlling for hardware variance.

This methodology is used for:
- Pre-merge/per-PR targeted perf checks (limited matrix, fast turnaround)
- Daily perf canary (expanded matrix, high confidence)
- Release qualification for runtime-1.22 tiered batching defaults

---

## Scope and non-goals
### In scope
...

title: Perf Canary: Synthetic Prompt Distribution (v0.4)
preview:
## Overview
This page defines the **synthetic prompt distribution** used by the production performance canary ("perf-canary"). The canary exists to detect **p95 latency regressions** and **tokens/sec drops** per model (optionally per region/tier/SKU) with minimal noise and zero customer data.

The prompt distributio...
text:
## Overview
This page defines the **synthetic prompt distribution** used by the production performance canary ("perf-canary"). The canary exists to detect **p95 latency regressions** and **tokens/sec drops** per model (optionally per region/tier/SKU) with minimal noise and zero customer data.

The prompt distribution is intentionally **shape-focused** (token lengths, prefill/decode mix, and request patterns) and uses **template-generated, non-sensitive content**.

**Version:** v0.4 (current)

---

## What changed since v0.3
This revision keeps the overall design and bucket structure, but updates several workload knobs to reduce variance and improve coverage of newer request paths.

Changes:
1. **Request interface mix updated** to include a small, strictly bounded **tool/function calling** shape.
2. **Bucket weights adjusted** to slightly increase mid/long-context coverage (P2/P3) whil...

title: Deterministic Playback Manifest Format (v1)
preview:
## Overview
This page defines the **Deterministic Playback Manifest** format (v1): a lightweight, self-contained JSON document intended to capture everything needed to deterministically replay an inference session (or an ordered set of requests) across model rollouts, runtime variants, kernel changes, and quantizati...
text:
## Overview
This page defines the **Deterministic Playback Manifest** format (v1): a lightweight, self-contained JSON document intended to capture everything needed to deterministically replay an inference session (or an ordered set of requests) across model rollouts, runtime variants, kernel changes, and quantization profiles.

The primary goal is to make postmortems, regression triage, and model A/B comparisons reproducible **without requiring full environment cloning**.

## Non-goals
The manifest is intentionally not a full provenance ledger.

**Out of scope for v1**
- Capturing private customer data inline. Manifests must reference redacted payloads or point to approved secure storage locations.
- General-purpose data lineage, training provenance, or governance/audit trails.

## Design principles
- **Minimal**: reference large artifacts instead of embedding blobs.
- **Deterministi...

title: Mixed-size Model Co-scheduling and Traceable Kernel Affinity Guide
preview:
Overview

This document describes a pragmatic design and operational playbook for co-scheduling mixed-size LLM inference requests (small models, medium backbone models, and large-context LLMs) on heterogeneous GPU pools while preserving predictable latency SLOs and providing traceable kernel-to-request affinity for ...
text:
Overview

This document describes a pragmatic design and operational playbook for co-scheduling mixed-size LLM inference requests (small models, medium backbone models, and large-context LLMs) on heterogeneous GPU pools while preserving predictable latency SLOs and providing traceable kernel-to-request affinity for incident investigation. It complements the scheduler language profiles and heterogeneous-pool guidance; its aim is prescriptive: how to set affinity, observe it end-to-end, and run safe rollouts.

Scope

- Applies to hosted and Dedicated clusters that share the same scheduler control plane.
- Covers inference-only workloads (token-generation and chat) and does not cover training jobs.
- Targets co-location of models with different memory/compute footprints on the same compute nodes and multi-model pipelines where a single request touches more than one model.

Motivation

Co...

title: quantization-rollout-safety-and-quality-gate-playbook
preview:
Summary
=======

This playbook defines a standardized, safety-first process for rolling out quantized model variants across Redwood's serving runtime. It balances unit-cost benefits from lower-precision weights with product-quality and reliability commitments. The document describes metrics, quality gates, phased ro...
text:
Summary
=======

This playbook defines a standardized, safety-first process for rolling out quantized model variants across Redwood's serving runtime. It balances unit-cost benefits from lower-precision weights with product-quality and reliability commitments. The document describes metrics, quality gates, phased rollout procedures, observability requirements, automated test harness commands, and rollback criteria.

Goals
-----
- Protect customer-facing SLOs and output quality while enabling quantization as a cost-optimization lever.
- Ensure reproducible validation and fast failure-detection for regressions introduced by quantization.
- Provide clear, actionable rollout steps for engineers, SREs, and product owners.

Scope
-----
Applies to: model variants served by the runtime (hosted, dedicated, private). This playbook covers post-onboarding quantized variants (post-model-onboarding...

title: Tiered-fallback-routing-and-eviction-architecture-2026
preview:
Overview:

This document describes the Tiered Fallback Routing and Eviction Architecture (TFREA) used by Redwood’s serving runtime to meet multi-dimensional SLOs (latency percentiles, cost budget, and availability) while degrading predictably during capacity pressure or downstream failure modes. TFREA unifies routin...
text:
Overview:

This document describes the Tiered Fallback Routing and Eviction Architecture (TFREA) used by Redwood’s serving runtime to meet multi-dimensional SLOs (latency percentiles, cost budget, and availability) while degrading predictably during capacity pressure or downstream failure modes. TFREA unifies routing policies, candidate model variants, eviction heuristics, and runtime-informed kernel scheduling hints into a single, observable control loop.

Goals:
- Maintain 99th-percentile latency SLOs per route under normal load.
- Bound hosted per-token cost within configured customer cost tiers.
- Provide deterministic, monotonic quality degradation (higher-quality -> lower-quality) with automated rollback when pressure subsides.
- Make fallback decisions explainable and traceable per request for post-incident analysis.

Scope:
- Applies to hosted and Dedicated modes; Private depl...

title: Graceful Restart Latency Contracts and Snapshot Synthesis
preview:
Overview

This document defines latency contracts (SLOs/SLO targets) and the operational protocol for graceful restarts of runtime workers, kernel modules, and model shards. It also specifies the snapshot synthesis format we use for resumable execution: what state is captured, how it is validated, and how resume/rol...
text:
Overview

This document defines latency contracts (SLOs/SLO targets) and the operational protocol for graceful restarts of runtime workers, kernel modules, and model shards. It also specifies the snapshot synthesis format we use for resumable execution: what state is captured, how it is validated, and how resume/rollback works. The goal is to provide a concrete, testable, and observable procedure that keeps customer-facing latency and correctness within predictable bounds during maintenance, upgrades, and transient failures.

Audience

- Runtime engineers implementing restart semantics and kernel handoff.
- SRE and oncall responsible for incident response when restarts impact SLOs.
- Release engineers and QA writing canary tests and fidelity benchmarks.

Definitions

- graceful restart: a coordinated shutdown and resume of a worker (or kernel) that preserves sufficient execution state...

title: Runtime safety: telemetry contracts and chaos-embargo plan
preview:
Summary:
This document defines a compact, operational telemetry contract for the serving runtime and the engineering policy we call the "chaos embargo"—time windows and gating rules that restrict disruptive experiments (kernel rollouts, hardware rebalancing, large-scale quantization flips, and canary expansion) unle...
text:
Summary:
This document defines a compact, operational telemetry contract for the serving runtime and the engineering policy we call the "chaos embargo"—time windows and gating rules that restrict disruptive experiments (kernel rollouts, hardware rebalancing, large-scale quantization flips, and canary expansion) unless safety conditions are met. The goal is to reduce blast radius during peak business hours and give SRE/Owners deterministic signal windows for fast assessment.

1) Why this exists
- Serving runtime changes interact with low-level GPU kernels and KV caching; small regressions can cause correlated tail-latency spikes and cross-tenant noise.
- We must guarantee experiments only proceed when essential health signals are stable, and we must have a fast path to freeze/rollback if we observe early regressions.

2) Scope and applicability
- Applies to all changes that can affect ...

title: Asynchronous Kernel Handoff Simulation and Safety Playbook
preview:
Summary

This playbook describes the simulation-driven, safety-first approach for introducing and validating asynchronous kernel handoffs in the runtime scheduler. It complements the scheduler contract and kernel handoff design docs by providing step-by-step simulation recipes, invariant checks, telemetry baselines,...
text:
Summary

This playbook describes the simulation-driven, safety-first approach for introducing and validating asynchronous kernel handoffs in the runtime scheduler. It complements the scheduler contract and kernel handoff design docs by providing step-by-step simulation recipes, invariant checks, telemetry baselines, and an incident recovery checklist for live rollouts.

Intended audience
- Runtime engineers implementing scheduler/handoff changes
- SREs validating rollouts and runbooks
- Performance engineers and profiling owners

Scope and definitions
- Asynchronous kernel handoff: transferring control of a kernel or execution slot from one worker / kernel instance to another without blocking incoming requests synchronously; may include prefetching of KV cache and lazy state reconciliation.
- Simulation harness: an isolated environment that replays production-like traces and injects f...

title: Runtime deployment circuit breakers and SLA fallback playbook
preview:
Purpose and scope:

This playbook documents the design and operational procedures for runtime deployment circuit breakers and SLA fallback strategies used by Redwood’s serving runtime. It standardizes automatic and manual interventions when serving infrastructure or model variants threaten customer SLOs. It is inten...
text:
Purpose and scope:

This playbook documents the design and operational procedures for runtime deployment circuit breakers and SLA fallback strategies used by Redwood’s serving runtime. It standardizes automatic and manual interventions when serving infrastructure or model variants threaten customer SLOs. It is intended for oncall SREs, runtime, and platform engineers who operate rollouts, incident response, and automated safety systems.

Background and goals:

- Circuit breakers protect global and per-tenant SLAs by detecting overload or degraded-quality conditions and triggering well-defined fallback actions.
- Fallbacks preserve availability first (serve simpler/cheaper model variant), then correctness and cost.
- Safety goals: minimize user-visible errors, keep p99 under SLO where possible, and bound cost/regressions from automatic remediation.

Key concepts and signals:

- Primary...

title: Cooperative Preemption and Warm-Start Playbook for Latency-SLO Workloads
preview:
Overview:
This playbook defines a cooperative preemption model and warm-start procedures for the Redwood serving runtime to preserve customer latency SLOs while maximizing GPU utilization. It consolidates scheduler signals, kernel boundary preemption, and KV/prefix cache warm-start techniques into an operationally s...
text:
Overview:
This playbook defines a cooperative preemption model and warm-start procedures for the Redwood serving runtime to preserve customer latency SLOs while maximizing GPU utilization. It consolidates scheduler signals, kernel boundary preemption, and KV/prefix cache warm-start techniques into an operationally safe procedure that on-call and platform engineers can follow during scale events, upgrades, and transient overloads.

Scope:
- Applies to GPU-backed serving nodes running the Redwood inference runtime (all major variants: hosted, dedicated, private).
- Focused on interactive text-generation and chat endpoints with strict P50/P95 latency SLOs (<= 50ms P50, <= 200ms P95 typical).
- Excludes bulk embedding/long-batch offline workloads except where co-located on shared pools (see co-location section).

Goals:
1. Maintain customer-visible latency SLOs during transient overloads ...

title: Execution Observer and Fallback Verification Manual

text:
Overview

Purpose: This manual describes the Execution Observer subsystem and the verification procedures for the runtime's fallback orchestration. The Execution Observer (exec-observer) is the deterministic diagnostic and verification component used in CI, canary rollouts, and incident replay to validate that fallback decisions preserve SLOs and maintain state correctness.

Scope: architecture, decision telemetry, verification harnesses, profiling scenarios, benchmarking profiles, runbook steps for validation and rollback, ownership, and acceptance criteria for changes to fallback policies and observer instrumentation.

Audience: runtime engineers, SREs, release engineers, and oncall responders.

1) System summary

What exec-observer is:
- A light-weight sidecar-style observer process that subscribes to the routing/decision events emitted by the runtime decision fabric.

title: Runtime incident orchestration and capacity decision matrix
preview:
Summary:
This playbook defines an operational decision matrix and step-by-step orchestration patterns for runtime incidents that span capacity, kernel scheduling regressions, model-fidelity degradation, and mixed-workload stress. It codifies: when to scale, when to switch model variants, which kernel tuning recipes ...
text:
Summary:
This playbook defines an operational decision matrix and step-by-step orchestration patterns for runtime incidents that span capacity, kernel scheduling regressions, model-fidelity degradation, and mixed-workload stress. It codifies: when to scale, when to switch model variants, which kernel tuning recipes to attempt, how to trigger targeted profiling and benchmarking, and rollback rules for safe recovery.

Scope and applicability:
- Applies to all production serving clusters running the Redwood serving runtime (hosted + dedicated pools).
- Relevant for incidents impacting latency SLOs, error rate regressions, GPU capacity saturation, and kernel scheduling anomalies.
- Not for control-plane-only incidents (see incident-process/control-plane-runbook).

Goals and SLOs referenced:
- Tier-1 latency SLO: 95th percentile request latency <= 250ms for short-chat shape (<=128 tokens)....

title: transient-model-switch-oscillation-incident-and-mitigation-2026
preview:
Summary:

This document describes a recurrent production phenomenon we observed in Q4 2025–Q1 2026 that we have labeled 'Transient Model Switch Oscillation' (T-MSO). Symptoms include a rapid sequence of model routing changes for the same logical request stream, causing repeated KV-cache invalidations, throughput col...
text:
Summary:

This document describes a recurrent production phenomenon we observed in Q4 2025–Q1 2026 that we have labeled 'Transient Model Switch Oscillation' (T-MSO). Symptoms include a rapid sequence of model routing changes for the same logical request stream, causing repeated KV-cache invalidations, throughput collapse, and elevated tail latency for 20–40s bursts. The behavior was visible across multiple tenants and both Hosted and Dedicated modes.

Purpose and audience:

- Audience: runtime engineers, oncall SREs, platform reliability, and product teams coordinating model rollout policies.
- Purpose: document root cause analysis, reproduction steps, mitigation and long-term fixes, and monitoring/alerting recipes.

Incident timeline and impact:

1) First escalations: 2025-11-12 — intermittent customer reports of long tail latencies (95th–99.9th spike) during midday traffic.
2) Patte...

text:
## Summary
Runtime **v1.22.0** ships the serving-runtime changes from the FP8/INT8 kernel benchmarking sprint.

Primary goals:
- Improve end-to-end latency/throughput for top hosted models by enabling **quantization-friendly kernels** (FP8 + INT8) where safe.
- Make kernel wins show up in production by tuning **prefill/decode scheduling** and adding better instrumentation.
- Reduce benchmark noise and rollout risk via improved metadata, perf-canary gating, and rollback tooling.

This release is designed for **staged enablement** (flags + per-model/per-region cohorts). Defaults remain conservative until perf canary + eval sign-off criteria are met (see “Rollout plan”).

---

## What changed (high level)
### 1) New/updated kernel paths
- **FP8 attention kernel integration (behind flag)**
 - Adds FP8 attention kernel path for supported GPU/shape regimes.
 - Includes safety fallback to ba...

title: Workload-aware batching defaults (Fast / Standard / Cost-efficient) — design
preview:
## Overview
This document defines Redwood Runtime’s “workload-aware batching defaults”: three customer-facing latency SLO tiers (Fast / Standard / Cost-efficient) implemented as tiered batching profiles selected automatically by the runtime based on workload signals and policy configuration.

The primary goal is to ...
text:
## Overview
This document defines Redwood Runtime’s “workload-aware batching defaults”: three customer-facing latency SLO tiers (Fast / Standard / Cost-efficient) implemented as tiered batching profiles selected automatically by the runtime based on workload signals and policy configuration.

The primary goal is to reduce cost per 1K tokens via safer batching defaults while preserving predictable tail latency (especially for streaming) and avoiding starvation under mixed workloads.

This is the primary design doc for:
- Tier definitions and the contract we uphold for each tier (in runtime terms).
- Scheduler knob mapping (continuous batching heuristics, queueing, prefill/decode balancing, admission control).
- Workload signals (length buckets, streaming flag, concurrency, overload signals) and selection logic.
- Override hierarchy (customer override > route/model policy > tier default...

title: Mixed-workload scheduler prototype: results summary (runtime-1.18 candidate)
preview:
## Summary
This page summarizes benchmark results for the **mixed-workload scheduler prototype** landing behind a feature flag in the **runtime-1.18 candidate**. The goal was to reduce **user-visible p95 tail latency** (especially TTFT for short prompts) under mixed prompt-length distributions without materially reg...
text:
## Summary
This page summarizes benchmark results for the **mixed-workload scheduler prototype** landing behind a feature flag in the **runtime-1.18 candidate**. The goal was to reduce **user-visible p95 tail latency** (especially TTFT for short prompts) under mixed prompt-length distributions without materially regressing throughput or fairness.

**Headline:** Across representative SKUs, the short/long class-based policy reduced **short-prompt p95 TTFT by ~18–41%** in mixed workloads, with **throughput within -0% to -3%** of baseline in most scenarios. Long-request latency increased slightly in some mixes but remained bounded with aging + token budgets. The primary regression observed was **kernel auto-selection instability on L40S** under a long-spike pattern (details below).

---

## Build + test matrix
**Candidate:** runtime-1.18.0-rc3 (scheduler prototype enabled via `scheduler.c...

text:
## Purpose
This runbook defines the operational procedure for enabling new inference kernels (e.g., FP8/INT8 attention + matmul paths) behind flags and rolling them out safely via perf canary before broader enablement.

The goals are:
- Detect latency/throughput regressions early (TTFT/TPOT/tokens/s) with enough statistical power.
- Minimize customer impact through cohorting, automatic rollback, and clear comms.
- Provide a repeatable, auditable checklist (what we changed, where it ran, what we observed).

## Scope
Applies to:
- Hosted API serving runtime rollouts (multi-tenant).
- Dedicated pools **only** when explicitly scheduled with Infra/SRE and customer success coordination.

Not covered:
- One-off benchmarking runs (see benchmark runner / sprint plan).
- Model catalog policy decisions (see quantization profile policy + ADR).

- Primary operational owner (S...

title: Runtime 1.21 Release Notes: Kernel Auto-Selection v2
preview:
## Summary
Runtime **1.21** introduces **Kernel Auto-Selection v2** ("selector v2") for GPU inference kernels. The selector chooses the best available kernel **per request** using a deterministic, explainable rule-based policy keyed on:
- **Model family / architecture** (e.g., Llama-like vs Mistral-like vs Qwen-like...
text:
## Summary
Runtime **1.21** introduces **Kernel Auto-Selection v2** ("selector v2") for GPU inference kernels. The selector chooses the best available kernel **per request** using a deterministic, explainable rule-based policy keyed on:
- **Model family / architecture** (e.g., Llama-like vs Mistral-like vs Qwen-like)
- **Sequence-length regime** (prefill-heavy, decode-heavy, mixed; plus long-context buckets)
- **GPU SKU** (e.g., A100 vs H100 vs L40S; including normalization of common cloud instance naming)

This release is focused on reducing **p95/p99 latency** and improving **throughput stability** across heterogeneous fleets without correctness regressions.

Selector v2 is shipped **behind feature gates** and includes fast rollback, per-model allow/deny controls, and guardrails for known-unstable driver/CUDA combinations.

---

## What’s New in 1.21
### 1) Kernel Auto-Selection v2 ...

title: Kernel Selection Architecture Overview (Serving Runtime)
preview:
## Overview
Kernel selection is the Serving Runtime subsystem responsible for choosing the concrete GPU implementation (“kernel plan”) for a request, given:
- Model identity and architecture/family
- Workload regime (prefill vs decode characteristics; sequence length buckets)
- Hardware target (GPU SKU, driver/CUDA ...
text:
## Overview
Kernel selection is the Serving Runtime subsystem responsible for choosing the concrete GPU implementation (“kernel plan”) for a request, given:
- Model identity and architecture/family
- Workload regime (prefill vs decode characteristics; sequence length buckets)
- Hardware target (GPU SKU, driver/CUDA capability, MIG partitioning)
- Runtime feature flags (quantization profile, attention variant, KV-cache layout)

This page describes:
1) Where selection happens in the execution pipeline
2) Interfaces to Scheduler and Compilation Cache
3) How decisions are cached, serialized, and made replayable/deterministic

This is an architectural overview. For the v2 decision logic and fallback policy, see ADR-017 and the v2 design doc.

---

## Goals
- Ensure **correctness-first** selection: only choose kernels that are compatible with the request + target hardware.
- Maintain **dete...

title: KV Cache Compaction Profiling Playbook
preview:

This page documents how to profile KV cache fragmentation and compaction behavior in the serving runtime. It focuses on:
- Capturing allocator state snapshots (fragmentation, freelists, per-sequence residency)
- Correlating compaction/eviction activity with tail latency (p95/p99) and scheduler phase bound...
text:
## Purpose
This page documents how to profile KV cache fragmentation and compaction behavior in the serving runtime. It focuses on:
- Capturing allocator state snapshots (fragmentation, freelists, per-sequence residency)
- Correlating compaction/eviction activity with tail latency (p95/p99) and scheduler phase boundaries
- Diagnosing “pathological fragmentation” cases (bursty churn, mixed sequence lengths, long-lived sessions)

This is an engineering playbook (not a runbook). If you are actively mitigating a production incident, follow the runtime operational runbook and use this page for deeper diagnosis.

## When to use this playbook
Use these steps when you see one or more of the following:
- p95/p99 decode latency spikes during burst traffic, especially with mixed prompt lengths
- Increased allocator overhead (alloc/free/defrag) or copy activity, without throughput gains
- Unexpec...

title: Kernel Auto-Selection v2 — Override and Rollback Runbook
preview:
## Purpose
This runbook describes how oncall can safely override or roll back **Kernel Auto-Selection v2** ("selector v2") when it causes tail-latency regressions, stability issues, or correctness risks. It covers:
- Disabling v2 by **model**, **GPU SKU**, and/or **region** (Hosted, Dedicated, Private)
- Pinning a s...
text:
## Purpose
This runbook describes how oncall can safely override or roll back **Kernel Auto-Selection v2** ("selector v2") when it causes tail-latency regressions, stability issues, or correctness risks. It covers:
- Disabling v2 by **model**, **GPU SKU**, and/or **region** (Hosted, Dedicated, Private)
- Pinning a specific kernel (temporary mitigation)
- Safe rollback procedure (fast + staged)
- Validation steps using dashboards and logs

**Primary goals:** restore p95/p99 latency and error rates to baseline while preserving correctness and preventing fleet-wide disruption.

---

## Scope / when to use
Use this runbook when any of the following are true during canary or production:
- p99 latency regression attributed to kernel selection (common: long-context prefill misclassification)
- Elevated runtime errors (CUDA illegal memory access, miscompiled kernel, launch failures) correlate...

text:
## Overview
This document defines the *runtime-exported inputs* required by the Standard Cost Model (SCM) to produce consistent per-request and aggregated cost breakdowns across Console, Optimize, and finance reporting.

The Serving Runtime is the source of truth for:
- Prefill vs decode token accounting (including accepted/emitted vs attempted tokens)
- KV cache behavior (hits/misses, reuse, evictions)
- Batching and scheduling outcomes (batch sizes, queue times, GPU occupancy proxies)
- Quantization execution path identifiers (what actually ran on-GPU)

This page is a contract between Serving Runtime, Telemetry, and the Cost Model service.

### Scope
**In scope (v1):** metrics emitted per request (or per request stage) that the cost model needs to explain cost deltas driven by caching/batching/quantization and to allocate runtime-attributable costs.

**Out of scope (v1):** full kern...

title: Runtime 1.22 Release Notes — KV Cache Compaction + Tier-Aware Eviction
preview:
## Summary
Runtime 1.22 introduces **KV cache compaction** and **tier-aware KV eviction** to reduce GPU memory fragmentation and mitigate burst-driven tail latency (p95/p99) for sustained decode workloads with mixed sequence lengths.

This release adds:
- A **fragmentation-aware allocator health model** (new fragmen...
text:
## Summary
Runtime 1.22 introduces **KV cache compaction** and **tier-aware KV eviction** to reduce GPU memory fragmentation and mitigate burst-driven tail latency (p95/p99) for sustained decode workloads with mixed sequence lengths.

This release adds:
- A **fragmentation-aware allocator health model** (new fragmentation metrics and allocator state tracking).
- **Budgeted compaction** (time/bytes) integrated with the scheduler to avoid decode stalls.
- A **tier-aware eviction policy** (LRU-by-sequence with safety constraints) to prevent opportunistic/chaotic evictions during bursts.
- **Tracing spans and exemplars** to correlate compaction/eviction events with tail latency.
- A **kill-switch** and rollout knobs to allow oncall to disable compaction/eviction quickly.

Primary expected impact:
- Lower tail latency variance during bursts and churn.
- Reduced incidence of “allocator thra...

title: Kernel Hint Propagation Profiling Pipeline
preview:
Summary:

This document specifies the Kernel Hint Propagation Profiling Pipeline (KHPPP) — an end-to-end process used by the serving runtime team to capture, synthesize, and propagate lightweight kernel hints from production traces to the kernel selection and scheduler subsystems. The pipeline is optimized for low-o...
text:
Summary:

This document specifies the Kernel Hint Propagation Profiling Pipeline (KHPPP) — an end-to-end process used by the serving runtime team to capture, synthesize, and propagate lightweight kernel hints from production traces to the kernel selection and scheduler subsystems. The pipeline is optimized for low-overhead sampling, deterministic replay for hypothesis testing, and safe automated rollout of hint-driven kernel recommendations to the runtime.

Goals:
- Reduce unexpected kernel regressions by surfacing causal correlations between request shape and kernel latency.
- Provide repeatable profiling artifacts usable by scheduler and kernel teams for autotune jobs.
- Automate a canary rollout path for kernel hint changes with observable safety gates.

Scope and non-goals:
- Scope: inference request-level sampling -> trace enrichment -> hint extraction -> validation replay -> rec...

title: Runtime Integration Gateway & Operator Playbook
preview:
Summary

This page documents the design, operational contract, and runbooks for the Runtime Integration Gateway (RIG) and the Operator component used by Redwood’s serving runtime. The RIG is the control plane facade that mediates decisions between the API surface, routing layer, and the kernel scheduler. The Operato...
text:
Summary

This page documents the design, operational contract, and runbooks for the Runtime Integration Gateway (RIG) and the Operator component used by Redwood’s serving runtime. The RIG is the control plane facade that mediates decisions between the API surface, routing layer, and the kernel scheduler. The Operator is the local agent that implements prefetch/eviction hints, capacity decisions, and safety checks on a per-host basis. Together they enable consistent fallback behavior, cache coordination, and safe kernel handoffs across deployments (hosted, dedicated, private).

Scope

- Cross-cutting: runtime architecture, kernel scheduling hints, profiling instrumentation, benchmark protocols, evacuation/eviction runbooks, and release guidance.
- Intended audience: runtime engineers, SREs, oncall, platform/ops teams, and partners owning Private deployments.

Design overview

Key goals...

title: Runtime change impact matrix & experimentation cookbook
preview:

This document defines a single, consistent process for assessing the operational impact of changes to the serving runtime and for running controlled experiments (feature gates, kernel rollouts, scheduler tuning, quantization profiles, and routing policies). It combines a lightweight impact matrix with an ...
text:
Overview:

This document defines a single, consistent process for assessing the operational impact of changes to the serving runtime and for running controlled experiments (feature gates, kernel rollouts, scheduler tuning, quantization profiles, and routing policies). It combines a lightweight impact matrix with an experimentation cookbook and the minimal runbook snippets required to triage regressions introduced by change. The goal is to reduce blast radius, speed detection, and make rollbacks predictable.

Scope:
- Applies to changes that modify: runtime binary (C++/CUDA), kernel selection mapping, scheduler policy, KV cache behavior, batching heuristics, and routing/fallback rules.
- Excludes purely client-side SDK changes or docs-only updates (those follow a different lightweight process).

Who should use this:
- Release engineers and runtime owners for planned rollouts
- SREs on-...

title: KV Cache Compaction Improvements — Design Doc (Runtime 1.22)
preview:
h1. Overview
This document proposes KV cache compaction + tier-aware eviction for Redwood’s serving runtime to reduce GPU memory fragmentation and mitigate burst-driven tail latency (p95/p99) during sustained decode workloads with mixed sequence lengths.

The design adds:
* A measurable definition of *fragmentation*...
text:
h1. Overview
This document proposes KV cache compaction + tier-aware eviction for Redwood’s serving runtime to reduce GPU memory fragmentation and mitigate burst-driven tail latency (p95/p99) during sustained decode workloads with mixed sequence lengths.

The design adds:
* A measurable definition of *fragmentation* for the KV arena
* A compaction mechanism (incremental, budgeted) integrated with the scheduler
* A safe eviction strategy (tier-aware LRU + pinned-session handling)
* Feature flags / kill-switches for staged rollout and rapid disable
* Observability standards (metrics + tracing) and rollout guardrails

h2. Context and problem statement
The runtime maintains a GPU-resident KV cache for active sequences (and optionally cached prefixes). Under mixed prompt lengths and high churn, long-lived allocations + frequent frees can create holes in the allocator arena. Over time, the ...

preview:

## Overview
This page defines the benchmark methodology and scenarios used to:
- Reproduce KV cache memory fragmentation under production-like decode workloads.
- Quantify the impact of the Runtime 1.22 KV compaction + eviction changes on p95/p99 tail latency.
- Prevent regressions to throughput and steady-state latency.
- Standardize pass/fail gates for CI perf checks, benchmark-lab runs, and perf-canary rollouts.

This plan is used by:
- Serving Runtime (allocator/compaction/scheduler work)
- SRE (canary guardrails and rollback criteria)
- Release train (runtime-1.22 readiness)

**Primary owners:** Rina Kobayashi (benchmarks), Marcus Reed (perf harness), Omar El-Sayed (KV cache).

---

## Definitions (used throughout)
**Fragmentation ratio (KV arena):** 1 - (largest_contiguous_free_bytes / total_free_bytes). Ranges [0, 1]. Higher = worse.

title: Runtime Optimization Config Apply Pipeline (Versioned Safe Apply + Rollback)
preview:
## Overview
This page describes how the Serving Runtime fetches, caches, and applies *versioned* Optimization Configs, and how it safely rolls back to a prior known-good version.

Optimization Configs control runtime tuning knobs that materially impact cost/latency/reliability, including:
- Batching heuristics (e.g....
text:
## Overview
This page describes how the Serving Runtime fetches, caches, and applies *versioned* Optimization Configs, and how it safely rolls back to a prior known-good version.

Optimization Configs control runtime tuning knobs that materially impact cost/latency/reliability, including:
- Batching heuristics (e.g., continuous batching targets, max batch tokens)
- Caching toggles and KV/prefix cache settings
- Quantization profile selection (where supported per model)

The runtime must support:
- **Versioned, immutable configs** (apply a specific version ID)
- **Idempotent application** (safe retries, safe duplicate events)
- **Partial rollout safety** (canary/percentage-based apply)
- **One-click rollback** with predictable time-to-mitigate

Non-goals for this document:
- Control plane data model and API details (covered in the Optimization Config Service page)
- Console UX flows (c...

title: Runtime Runbook: KV Cache Compaction + Tier-Aware Eviction
preview:
## Purpose
This runbook describes how to operate **KV cache compaction** and **tier-aware eviction** in the serving runtime. It is intended for **runtime on-call** and **SRE** during canaries and broad rollouts.

Compaction reduces KV allocator fragmentation under mixed sequence lengths + churn. Eviction provides a ...
text:
## Purpose
This runbook describes how to operate **KV cache compaction** and **tier-aware eviction** in the serving runtime. It is intended for **runtime on-call** and **SRE** during canaries and broad rollouts.

Compaction reduces KV allocator fragmentation under mixed sequence lengths + churn. Eviction provides a controlled pressure release to prevent opportunistic, worst-time evictions (or OOM/shedding) during bursts.

This runbook covers:
- Verifying compaction/eviction is enabled (and at what settings)
- Reading core metrics and dashboards
- Common failure modes and triage
- Adjusting budgets/thresholds safely
- Emergency kill-switch and rollback steps

## Scope / systems
**Applies to:**
- Runtime version: **runtime-1.22+** (feature-flagged)
- Deployments: Hosted API pools, Dedicated GPU pools, and Private (where enabled)

**Core components involved:**
- KV arena allocator (pages...

title: Runtime failure inoculation and experimentation manual
preview:
Overview

This document defines Redwood's recommended practice for failure inoculation experiments against the serving runtime. The goal is to run controlled, measurable experiments that surface weak points in: kernel scheduling, KV-cache consistency, model swap safety, preemption and eviction, cross-tenant interfer...
text:
Overview

This document defines Redwood's recommended practice for failure inoculation experiments against the serving runtime. The goal is to run controlled, measurable experiments that surface weak points in: kernel scheduling, KV-cache consistency, model swap safety, preemption and eviction, cross-tenant interference, and network/service mesh degradations — while preserving customer SLAs and ensuring fast rollback.

Scope and motivation

- Scope: hosted, dedicated, and private runtime topologies. Focus area for this manual: experiments that exercise the inference serving stack layers (dispatcher, scheduler, kernel, kvcache, model loader, and network fabric).
- Motivation: proactively discover degradations and channel findings into kernel/scheduler hardening, autoscaling rules, runtime admission policies, and fidelity fallback contracts.

Definitions

- Failure inoculation: delibera...
